query: VHS scanlines TV static analog distortion CRT screen
message: sub:analog_degradation/VHS_CRT — anti_aesthetic — scanlines, TV static, color bars, pixel grids, retro CRT

response.objective_caption: A macro photograph of an illuminated digital display screen showing vertical columns of red, green, and blue subpixels across the entire frame. In the center, horizontal rows of blocky characters or symbols are rendered by clusters of illuminated and dark pixels, creating a light pattern against the dense multicolored grid.
response.anti_aesthetic_caption: A macro photograph of an illuminated digital display screen where aggressive red, green, and blue vertical stripes clash across the frame, foregrounding aliasing and the digital substrate through exposed subpixel structure. Horizontal rows of blocky characters appear at center, rendered in raw pixelated form, with the dense grid of conflicting hues emphasizing the materiality of the digital medium over seamless representation.
